summary.intent: Automate GitLab MR approval rule management for the release-from-fbc pipeline by adding an mr_approvers field to ocp-build-data group.yml and updating art-tools to configure approval rules after creating shipment MRs. First tested manually by adding a QE approval group to an existing MR using CLI tools.
summary.outcome: Successfully added QE approval rule to MR !453 and removed unwanted groups using curl/GitLab API. Implemented full automation: added JSON schema support, GitLabClient.set_mr_approval_rules method, pipeline integration in release_from_fbc.py, and mr_approvers field to logging-6.5 group.yml. Fixed dry-run and type validation bugs.

repo: ocp-build-data group.yml supports custom fields like mr_approvers for per-group configuration
art-tools uses JSON schema validation with additionalProperties: false, requiring explicit property additions
release-from-fbc pipeline follows _load_*_from_group_config pattern using doozer to read group.yml
Project-level GitLab approval rules are inherited by MRs and can't be fully deleted at MR level, only overridden
The pipeline uses placeholder URLs during dry-run mode that fail if API resolution is attempted

code.finding: During dry-run, mr_url is a placeholder string that causes API calls to fail; must check self.dry_run before calling methods that resolve URLs
stdlib_yaml.safe_load can return non-dict types (string, list, int); must validate isinstance(result, dict) before returning to callers expecting dict

GitLabClient pattern uses self.dry_run checks and self._client (python-gitlab library) for API operations; python-gitlab supports MR-level approval rules natively via mr.approval_rules.list/create/delete

workflow: glab CLI has 'glab api' for raw GitLab REST API calls but doesn't support MR approval rule management directly
GitLab API requires URL-encoded project paths (e.g., hybrid-platforms%2Fart%2Focp-shipment-data)
glab api has issues with array parameter encoding (user_ids[]), requiring fallback to curl with JSON body
python-gitlab library (already a dependency in art-tools) provides native support for approval rules
Git workflow for art-tools/ocp-build-data: fetch prod, create branch from prod branch, push to origin (personal fork), raise PR to prod

friction: glab api failed to properly encode array parameters (user_ids[]) when creating approval rules, requiring curl with JSON body instead
GitLab API returned 409 conflict initially due to existing 'any-approver' rule type
Project-level inherited approval rules appeared to persist after deletion because they can't be removed at MR level
Dry-run mode used placeholder MR URLs that caused failures when API methods tried to resolve them before checking dry_run flag
YAML parsing could return non-dict types but wasn't validated, causing potential downstream issues
open_items: The implementation was tested only with logging-6.5; other logging-* branches mentioned in initial request weren't updated
The mr_approvers feature is now generic but only configured for one group (logging-6.5) - expansion to other groups is pending
The JSON schema allows any object for mr_approvers without validating the structure (keys are strings, values are arrays of strings)
art-tools changes were committed and pushed to PR, but ocp-build-data PR creation/push wasn't completed
